welcome_p1: This test measures your ability to track multiple moving objects simultaneously. It assesses visual attention, concentration, and processing speed.
welcome_p2: You will see several objects on the screen. Some will be briefly highlighted as targets. After they start moving, your task is to keep track of the targets and identify them once they stop.
tutorial_step1_text: At the start of each trial, some objects will flash red. These are your targets. Memorize their positions.
tutorial_step2_text: All objects will become the same color and start moving around the screen. Follow the targets with your eyes as they move.
tutorial_step3_text: When the objects stop moving, click on the ones you believe were the original targets. Click an object again to deselect it.
tutorial_step4_text: Once you have selected the correct number of targets, press the submit button. You will see feedback showing which selections were correct.
demo_intro: Watch how a trial works step by step.
demo_step1: The red objects are your targets. Memorize them!
demo_step2: Now all objects become the same color...
demo_step3: The objects are moving! Follow the targets with your eyes.
demo_step4: The objects have stopped. Click the ones you think were targets.
demo_step5: Green = correct, red = missed target, orange = wrong selection.
demo_step6: You understand how it works. Ready to practice?
practice_complete_text1: Great job! You have completed the practice.
practice_complete_text2: In the real test, keep your eyes on the targets as they move. Try to be as accurate as possible.
countdown_instruction: Track the highlighted objects with your eyes
settings_total_trials_desc: Total number of trials in the test
settings_num_targets_desc: Objects to track (max half of total)
settings_identification_duration_desc: How long targets are highlighted in red
settings_tracking_duration_desc: How long objects move before stopping
settings_show_feedback_desc: Display correct/incorrect results after each response
footer_description: Track multiple moving objects simultaneously to measure visual attention.
